abstract: The education system in Indonesia is undergoing constant transformation as it navigates the demands of a globalized era. This study explores the critical relationship between education and national competitiveness, particularly in light of Indonesia's fluctuating performance in international rankings. Through a comprehensive literature review and descriptive analysis of academic journals, policy documents, and expert perspectives, this paper identifies education as a foundational pillar for bolstering human resource quality. The findings underscore that educational equity and quality are not merely social goals but economic imperatives; they are the primary mechanisms through which individuals develop the skills, character, and innovative capacity required to compete globally. Furthermore, the article examines recent policy shifts—such as "Kampus Merdeka" and the restructuring of final project requirements—as strategic attempts to align academic outcomes with the practical needs of the labor market, ultimately positioning education as the ultimate determinant of a nation’s standing on the world stage.
